name: Teradata MCP Server
what_it_does: Official Teradata MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with Teradata Vantage enterprise data warehouses — executing SQL and Teradata-specific analytical SQL, querying data warehouse schemas and tables, accessing Teradata's advanced analytics functions, and integrating enterprise-scale data warehousing into agent-driven analytics and business intelligence workflows.
use_cases: Executing analytical SQL queries against Teradata Vantage from BI agents
Querying enterprise data warehouse schemas from data discovery agents
Accessing Teradata advanced analytics functions from ML pipeline agents
Generating SQL for complex multi-table joins from natural language query agents
Monitoring data warehouse performance and usage from ops agents
Integrating Teradata data into LLM-powered analytics workflows
not_for: Teams without Teradata Vantage access (large enterprise licensing)
Small databases or OLTP workloads (Teradata is for large-scale analytics)
Teams using other data warehouses (Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift require different MCPs)
best_when: An enterprise analytics team with Teradata Vantage needs AI-assisted query generation and data exploration — particularly for complex analytical workloads on petabyte-scale data warehouses.
avoid_when: You use Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, or don't have Teradata infrastructure.

auth.notes: Teradata credentials (host, username, password) required. Teradata may use LDAP/Kerberos for enterprise auth. teradatasql Python driver used. Connection string via environment variables.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Teradata Vantage Express: free VM edition for dev/test
pricing.paid_tiers: Teradata enterprise: significant enterprise licensing costs
pricing.notes: Teradata is a major enterprise expense. Vantage Express free VM available for development. Official MCP from Teradata — well-maintained. ClearScape Analytics trial available on teradata.com.

performance.rate_limits: Enterprise Teradata — no MCP rate limits; query complexity bound

known_agent_gotchas: Teradata SQL dialect has differences from ANSI SQL — agents need Teradata-specific syntax
LDAP/Kerberos auth common in enterprise — may complicate setup vs simple user/pass
Teradata teradatasql Python driver must be installed — separate pip install
Official Teradata MCP — reflects Teradata's investment in AI integration
Column case sensitivity differs from other SQL databases
Long-running analytical queries can timeout — set appropriate timeouts
agent_readiness.security_notes: Enterprise credentials. TLS encryption supported. Use read-only Teradata user for analytics agents. Official Teradata MCP. SOC2/HIPAA/PCI compliant. Store credentials in env var.

security_score_components.security_notes: Enterprise auth. TLS. Read-only user for agents. SOC2/HIPAA/PCI. Official Teradata MCP. Store creds securely.
